summary: Full authoritative task reference lives at C:\Obsidian\Hivemind\30 Library\Task Management System.md — it covers every field, state, tag, date marker, and workflow rule in exhaustive detail.

Canonical task form: `- [STATE] PRIORITY Description ⏳/📅 YYYY-MM-DD #tag` — all fields after state and description are optional, but priority + date are required for any active task in a daily/weekly note.

Two date markers serve distinct roles: ⏳ (scheduled) = intent to work, default for all tasks, updated on carry-forward; 📅 (due) = hard external deadline, never updated on carry-forward, reserved for performance reviews / QA windows / release dates.

Four system-level hashtags exist: #waiting (must pair with ⏳, excluded from Today/This Week/Upcoming views), #someday (no date, excluded from all date-based views), #theirs (other person's work, hard-excluded from all personal views, never carried forward), #agenda (meeting-only, excluded from Today/This Week).

New Google Tasks items should land in daily notes (00 Daily Plan/YYYY-MM-DD.md) under the Top Priorities section using ⏳ scheduled dates, or in project notes (20 Projects/) under a Work Plan section — never injected directly into the weekly plan.

findings.key_finding: Canonical task syntax is `- [STATE] PRIORITY Description DATEMARKER YYYY-MM-DD #tag`. Position is strict: state first, priority immediately after the checkbox, description, then date marker + ISO date, then optional tags anywhere on the line. Priority is required for tasks in daily/weekly notes; tasks without priority won't sort predictably in dashboard views.

⏳ (scheduled) = 'I intend to work on this date' — default for all project tasks and carry-forwards. 📅 (due) = hard external deadline with real consequences for missing it. Skills always default to ⏳. On carry-forward, ⏳ dates are updated to today/tomorrow; 📅 dates are frozen. The distinction is semantically load-bearing for the dashboard query logic.

Five checkbox states: [ ] open, [x] done, [/] in-progress (pulled into daily from weekly plan), [>] deferred (carried to next day), [-] dropped. The extended states [/], [>], [-] are treated as 'not done' by obsidian-tasks plugin and appear in queries. They serve as an audit trail — patching source notes prevents the same task appearing as open in multiple consecutive daily notes.

Four system-level tags: #waiting (blocked, must have ⏳ follow-up date, visible in Waiting dashboard only), #someday (no date, visible only in Someday/Maybe view), #theirs (tracks another person's work, hard-excluded from ALL personal views and carry-forwards, used only in quarterly notes for report goals), #agenda (meeting items, excluded from Today/This Week/Upcoming). All other tags are personal and have no skill-level behavior.

Three priority markers: ⏫ (high/hard commit, max 3 per day), 🔼 (medium/should-do), 🔽 (low/nice-to-have). The 3-commit rule is enforced by /plan-today. Priority marker appears after the checkbox, before the description text.

/plan-today scaffolds 00 Daily Plan/YYYY-MM-DD.md each morning pulling from: (1) carry-forward tasks from yesterday's log, (2) weekly plan tasks not yet picked up, (3) QA issue tasks from in-testing DLGs. New Google Tasks synced to the vault should therefore target the daily note's Top Priorities section or a project note's Work Plan section — not injected directly into the weekly plan.

Seven dashboard query sections exist: Today (⏳ or 📅 on today, excludes #theirs/#someday/#waiting/#agenda and Quarterly path), This Week (⏳ or 📅 this week, same exclusions minus #waiting), Overdue (📅 before today), Waiting (#waiting tag), Someday/Maybe (#someday tag), Upcoming 14 days (⏳ or 📅 in next 14 days), Report Goals (#theirs AND Quarterly meeting path). Google Tasks items must be compatible with these queries to surface correctly.

Daily note template uses type: journal frontmatter. Top Priorities section has an inline comment documenting the full task syntax: priority markers, date markers, tag meanings, and checkbox states. The template itself only pre-populates two empty checkboxes — actual tasks are added by /plan-today or the user. Google Tasks should populate this section.

Weekly plan notes use type: weekly-plan frontmatter (note: vault-config.json doesn't list this as a valid controlled vocabulary for 'type', but actual weekly plan notes use type: journal per W14 plan). In weekly plans, 📅 is used only in the Hard Commitments section; ⏳ is used for all project tasks. The Hard Commitments section uses 📅 because calendar commitments are hard external constraints.

Manual task placement rules: daily note Top Priorities = today's work; weekly plan Projects section = this week's project work; project primary doc Work Plan section = ongoing project tasks; quarterly note = #theirs goals for direct reports (using 📅). Google Tasks items are most analogous to 'today's work' or 'project work' and should target daily notes or project notes accordingly.

Real daily notes confirm the syntax: `- [ ] ⏫ Description 📅 YYYY-MM-DD — annotation #tag` pattern. Notes also show inline freetext annotations (e.g., 'blocked: waiting on John/Sandeep') after the date marker. Completion dates use ✅ YYYY-MM-DD appended to the line.

No dedicated inbox folder exists (the 99 inbox directory listed in the vault tree appears to be empty or unused as a task inbox). There is no capture/inbox processing flow defined — tasks go directly into their target note (daily, weekly, or project). The vault-config.json documents folder paths and frontmatter schema but no inbox routing.

Example of a #waiting + #theirs combination on a task with no date: `- [L] Awaiting John Leonard's updated approach specs — no action this week #waiting #theirs`. This shows tags can be combined. The [L] priority notation in older notes is the legacy format (pre-v2.0); current system uses ⏫/🔼/🔽 emoji. Both exist in the vault; new tasks should use emoji.

gaps: No explicit capture/inbox flow exists for external task sources (like Google Tasks). There is no 'inbox.md' file or defined routing for inbound tasks — the system assumes tasks are created directly in context-appropriate notes. A sync tool will need to decide the target note (daily vs project) at insertion time.
The vault has no deduplication mechanism documented. If Google Tasks are synced repeatedly, the system provides no built-in protection against duplicate task lines in notes.

No tag exists for 'source: google-tasks' or any external provenance tagging. The tag taxonomy is closed at four system tags; any source tracking for synced tasks would need a new convention (or be encoded in the description text).
The 99 inbox directory appears in the vault directory listing but its contents were empty — it's unclear if this was ever used as a capture inbox or is vestigial.

The obsidian-tasks plugin version (v7.23.1) was mentioned but its exact query syntax variants (e.g., whether 'scheduled on today' vs 'scheduled today' are both valid) were not confirmed from plugin docs.
Recurrence syntax is mentioned as supported by obsidian-tasks but no examples of recurring task syntax appear in any vault note or template reviewed.
delta_queries: Search vault for any use of 🔁 (recurrence emoji) to find recurring task examples: grep -r '🔁' 'C:\Obsidian\Hivemind'
Check if 99 inbox is used by any skill: grep -r '99 inbox' 'C:\EpicSource\Projects\obsidian-work-process'
Search for any existing Google Tasks or external sync references in vault: vault_search 'google tasks sync' or 'tasks sync'
Read C:\Obsidian\Hivemind\10 Planning\Goals.md to understand if project-level goal tasks use a different syntax
Check 20 Projects for a project primary doc example with Work Plan section to confirm project task syntax in context
Check if /plan-today skill source code enforces specific date marker logic when creating tasks from external sources

values.[/]: in-progress — pulled from weekly plan into daily note by /plan-today
values.[>]: deferred — carried forward to next day by /debrief or /plan-today
values.[-]: dropped — consciously closed, will not be done

priority_marker.required: yes, for tasks in daily/weekly notes

values.⏫: high / hard commit — max 3 per day

description.notes: free text; wikilinks [[like this]] encouraged for people/projects

values.⏳: scheduled — intent to work on this date; DEFAULT; updated on carry-forward; use for all project tasks and carry-forwards
values.📅: due — hard external deadline; NEVER updated on carry-forward; reserve for performance reviews, QA windows, release dates, calendar commitments

completion_date.position: appended after date value when task is completed
completion_date.notes: written by /debrief when marking tasks done

system_tags.#waiting: blocked on someone/something external; MUST have ⏳ date (follow-up check date); visible only in Waiting dashboard view; excluded from Today/This Week/Upcoming
system_tags.#someday: parked indefinitely; NO date; visible only in Someday/Maybe dashboard view; excluded from all date-based views
system_tags.#theirs: tracks another person's work; NEVER carry forward; excluded from ALL personal dashboard views; only used in quarterly notes for report goals
system_tags.#agenda: item to raise with a person in a meeting; excluded from Today and This Week dashboard views

tags.notes: All other tags are personal/organizational and have no skill-level behavior

where_tasks_live.weekly_plan_hard_commitments: calendar-bound hard commitments — 10 Planning/YYYY-Www Weekly Plan.md; use 📅
where_tasks_live.weekly_plan_projects: this week's project work — same file, Projects section; use ⏳
where_tasks_live.project_primary_doc: ongoing project tasks — 20 Projects/<Name>/<Name>.md Work Plan section
where_tasks_live.quarterly_note: #theirs goals for direct reports — 07 Meetings/Quarterly/...; use 📅

dashboard_queries.Today: scheduled on today OR due on today; excludes #theirs, #someday, #waiting, #agenda, Quarterly path
dashboard_queries.This Week: scheduled this week OR due this week; excludes #theirs, #someday, #agenda, Quarterly
dashboard_queries.Waiting: tags include #waiting; sorted by scheduled date
dashboard_queries.Upcoming 14 days: scheduled or due in next 14 days; excludes #theirs, #someday, #agenda, Quarterly
dashboard_queries.Report Goals: tags include #theirs AND path includes Quarterly; for direct report goal tracking only

recommended_format_for_google_tasks_sync.notes: Use ⏳ by default. Only use 📅 if the Google Task has a hard external deadline. Assign 🔼 as default priority unless the task is explicitly urgent (⏫) or low-stakes (🔽). Target 00 Daily Plan/YYYY-MM-DD.md for tasks due today; consider a project note for tasks associated with a known project.
